title: Pabstiella campestris
taxonomy: Pabstiella campestris belongs to the family Pleurothallidinae and is a species of orchid. More specifically, it is part of the genus Pabstiella, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. Within this genus, Pabstiella campestris is classified as Pabstiella campestris (Luer) Toscano. This classification places it within the subfamily Epidendroideae, a large and diverse group of orchids. The combination of these classifications provides a comprehensive understanding of the plant's taxonomic position.

intro: Pabstiella campestris, also known as the Pabstiella species, is a type of orchid native to South America, particularly in the tropical regions of Brazil and Argentina. This orchid has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pabstiella campestris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pabstiella campestris is often prepared as a tea or tincture to alleviate respiratory symptoms, as well as to treat digestive issues and infections.
